title: CompTIA Network+ Certified
description: Validates end-to-end networking knowledge: design, configuration, management, and troubleshooting — across wired and wireless environments. Vendor-neutral, meaning the skills apply across any stack, not just one vendor's ecosystem. The baseline for any serious network security role.

title: CompTIA Security+ Certified
description: The global baseline for cybersecurity competency. Covers how attacks work, how defenses fail, and how to build systems that hold. Recognized by the U.S. Department of Defense under Directive 8570. Passing it means understanding threats from both sides of the perimeter.

title: Network Security Fundamentals
description: Palo Alto Networks builds firewalls deployed in some of the world's most demanding network environments. This certification covers their next-generation firewall architecture — application-layer inspection, threat prevention, URL filtering, and user-based policies. Practical context for the tools running in enterprise and government networks.

title: Advent of Cyber 2025
description: A 24-day intensive cybersecurity challenge covering the full attack-defense spectrum. Topics included Linux exploitation, web application attacks, network enumeration, log analysis, cloud security, and AI threat modeling. Completed every challenge — proof of breadth across core cybersecurity domains, not just depth in one area.
